parsed_review.summary: CP00-418 is a descriptor-only payments-core pack covering the 10 RP13.P09.M06 "Synthetic Fixture Set" rows (RP13.P09.M06.S02-S11, risk A). Plan (closeout-pack-plan.json:411), manifest binding, PAYMENTS_CORE_CP418_PACK_BINDING (registry.js:443), and contract current_pack (payments-core-contract.json:16) are fully consistent on pack_id/risk/unit_count/range/upstream CP00-417/next CP00-419/next_subphase S12, and the deliverable distribution {claude_review:1,security_audit:2,test:1,ui:1,implementation:5}=10 and micro_title {Synthetic Fixture Set:10} match. The production_ready claim is honestly false (runtime_ready false, implementation_layer descriptor); production_ready_flag is a descriptor-verification token, not a runtime claim, so no gate is weakened. Descriptor-only/no-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced in depth: service.js freezeCp418Result (line 3286) forces all runtime/dispatch/write flags false with synthetic_only/no_real_data true, and validators.js validatePaymentsCoreCp418P09FixtureSliceDescriptor (line 4746) rejects any row not descriptor_only/runtime_execution:false/customer_safe_errors_only/raw_payload_included:false, plus drift on Claude/enterprise-trust boundaries. Claude is held to read-only non-approver: authority_boundary.claude_is_final_approval=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false, asserted by tests (model.test.js:1789-1823) and the contract validator. Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic evidence packet/receipt (receipt_shape "...no_write", emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false), not product runtime. command-evidence.json records all node --check/--test, rpXX, plan/weighted/spec/control-plane/goal/fullplan validates, npm test, npm run validate, and git diff --check at exit 0; readiness auth-preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON (CP178+ hardening satisfied). index.js re-exports CP418 via export * (explains it being unmodified), and the README#cp00-418 entry is accurate. The not-yet-existing claude-review-result.json/adjudication.md/construction-inspection.json is the expected pre-review state (step 3 of the 9-step hardening sequence), not a defect. No P0/P1/P2 issues found; one P3 informational note on redundant scope-list labeling. Pack closeout and goal closeout are not blocked. Note per authority boundary: Claude is a read-only independent reviewer and not the final approver; human final approval remains required for any enterprise-trust claim.

findings.title: changed-file-scope lists two untracked paths in both implementation and unrelated buckets
findings.details: docs/closeout-pack-plan/new-session-handoff.md and docs/closeout-packs/cp00-418/ each appear in both untracked_implementation_files (lines 130-133) and unrelated_dirty_files_preserved (lines 135-145), which are intended to be mutually exclusive. This is a scope-generator labeling artifact. It has no effect on active_pack_implementation_refs (the 8 payments files: contract, README, index.js, registry.js, service.js, validators.js, model.test.js, validator script), the descriptor implementation, or any production_ready/no-write boundary, and active_untracked_implementation_files_omitted is empty.
findings.recommendation: Optionally de-duplicate the scope classification so each untracked path appears in exactly one bucket. Non-blocking; no impact on pack or goal closeout.
